codeReview.summary: The feature adds dedicated approved-stack coverage for VAL-VISUAL-016 and updates the main stale ASCII-era note in immersive-visual-language.test.tsx. I found no blocking defects, but some adjacent tests still use stale Hermes-era terminology, so the coverage language is not fully normalized to the new stack.

issues.description: This describe block still frames the site-wide atmosphere as 'Radiant/Hermes', which leaves old ASCII/Hermes stack language in related coverage after this cleanup. Similar stale terminology also remains in src/app/__tests__/logotype-theme-atmosphere.test.tsx:7 and src/components/visual/__tests__/direct-source-provenance.test.tsx:9/96.

sharedStateObservations.observation: frontend-experience-worker step 14 is too rigid for pure test-only or coverage-update features. This worker had to mark followedProcedure=false even though the feature correctly added coverage for already-shipped behavior.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: frontend-experience-worker/SKILL.md:33 requires failing tests first; the handoff JSON at lines 100-109 records followedProcedure=false and explicitly suggests clarifying test-only/coverage-update handling.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance is inconsistent on VAL-VISUAL-016: the feature description says the contract was already tightened to the approved stack, but validation-contract.md still defines VAL-VISUAL-016 as an ASCII/Hermes renderer. The worker transcript shows this mismatch caused confusion during implementation review.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md:281-283 still says 'ASCII layer uses a real-time Hermes-derived canvas renderer'; the transcript skeleton entry at worker-transcripts.jsonl:101 notes that the contract 'still has' the old ASCII wording before the worker proceeded.

summary: Pass. The commit adds the intended approved-stack test coverage for VAL-VISUAL-016 and updates the main stale comment, with no blocking defects found. One non-blocking cleanup gap remains: several adjacent tests still use stale Hermes-era terminology. Shared-state follow-up: clarify the skill's test-first rule for test-only features and resolve the VAL-VISUAL-016 contract wording mismatch.
